item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure if I've found an issue or if something has completely gone over my head, but all of the emails have the same date and time?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While the punchline was good and forced air out of my nose, I saw it coming from a mile away with Daniel's second email. If you tweak it a bit so it doesn't say the punchline 5 paragraphs late, I might upvote, but for now, no vote.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Okay, let me explain.

I searched up deflated balloon dog, and I found this site: https://blog.christinewongyap.com/tag/balloon-dog/

Made by Christine Wong Yap. However, the site obtained the image from this site:
http://stevenwolffinearts.com/dynamic/exhibit_artist.asp?ExhibitID=115

Which no longer works. In fact, http://stevenwolffinearts.com/ no longer works. A quick search for Steven Wolf Fine Arts, says that it's permanently closed. Instead, I get this site:
https://openspace.sfmoma.org/author/steven-wolf/, which has embeded links to http://stevenwolffinearts.com/.

https://openspace.sfmoma.org/ gives permission for usage.

Except as expressly authorized by SFMOMA and/or the applicable rights holder, or except for parts of the Service expressly subject to a separate license (in which case such license will govern use of that part of the Service), the Materials are made available for limited noncommercial, educational, and personal use only, or for fair use as defined in United States copyright laws. Additional terms or restrictions may be specified in the metadata or SFMOMA policies applicable to the Materials. You must, however, cite the author and source of the Materials as you would material from any printed work, and the citations should include the URL “www.sfmoma.org.” By downloading, printing, or otherwise using Materials, whether accessed directly from the Service or via other sites, services or mechanisms, you agree that you will limit your use of such files to noncommercial, educational, or personal use, or for fair use, and will not violate the SFMOMA’s or any other party’s proprietary rights or applicable policies. You may not remove any copyright, trademark, or other proprietary notices, including without limitation attribution information, credits, and copyright notices that have been placed on or near the Materials by SFMOMA.

As far as I have researched, https://blog.christinewongyap.com/tag/balloon-dog/ is the only site that still has the image, but doesn't cite the source in any way.

To paraphrase, Jeffery Songco's work was photographer by an unknown photographer and posted on a now defunct website of the museum the work is at. The picture was used in a blog, where I got it, but the museum's website was deleted, making the blog download of the original image the copy I used. The owner of the museum is a member of a group that allows use of is images in personal, educational, and non-commercial use.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kind of executed a bit weird, and I'm not really a big fan of how the joke was executed. I do see how the idea could be appealing with more work. Novote.

Also those email URLs really bug me, that's not how domains work. The TLD (top-level domain, like .com or .org or whatever) isn't going to be the department, either the Foundation has registered .scp, or they use the technically more correct but more boring .internal for private/internal networks. So your domains would be something like antimeme.foundation.scp and raisa.foundation.scp (or maybe their own domains, e.g. raisa.scp).

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Ok, is that good? The image linked to that site, and, I quote:

Having a quote about existing collaborators (which you are not listed as, it seems?) does not equate to permission being given for creative commons usage of these images. You would need to provide licensing information or direct permission from the creator of the image.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Oh sorry

Ok, is that good? The image linked to that site, and, I quote:

Our collaborators are critical and creative practitioners

But I found the image on https://blog.christinewongyap.com/tag/balloon-dog/.

Jeffrey Songco is the artist who made the artwork…couldn't find the photographer

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Currently, you have not sourced the image used in this article, and as such may be a violation of the SCP wiki's license (CC-BY-SA 3.0). You have 24 hours to source the image used. If you have not sourced the image within the allotted time frame, or the source is noncompliant with the SCP wiki's license, then we'll have to take the image down.

EDIT: While the image has been properly sourced, the image itself is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license, as it allows for images to be used for non-commercial purposes, making the license include "NC" which disobeys CC-BY-SA 3.0. Therefore, the image has been removed. As a note, you do not need to cite your image in-article (besides an info panel), just in the discussion page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent Wordplay and an original concept. Easy +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You forgot to replace all the XXXXs with 4937

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yeah sorry, I was trying to work out the tags system because I don't have much experience.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Author, I've cleared all non-contest tags. If you're not going to add all the correct the tags for your article, it's best to just leave the page untagged and wait for an experienced tagger to take care of it. We have a team of people on the site who tag articles, and they search for pages that have no tags. Adding the incorrect tags prevents the team from finding the article, so if you're unfamiliar with the tags, it's totally fine to wait for them to take care of it.

In this case, you added the tags scp, euclid, canine, however, your article may require more tags beyond these three.
